item.title: After ‘Sausage Making,’ European Lawmakers Approve U.S. Trade Deal
item.description: The trade deal that President Trump struck with the European Union has cleared a major hurdle that delayed it for months.

item.title: Could New York’s climate rethink spread to other blue states?
item.description: Democratic officials are reassessing targets as concerns grow over energy affordability

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Says Iranian Naval Commander Is Killed in Airstrike
item.description: Israel’s defense minister said that Alireza Tangsiri, a key player in Iran’s de facto blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, was dead. Iran has not commented on his condition.

item.title: NS&I set to pay millions to customers over misplaced funds
item.description: The government-backed bank has been accused of a series of errors, including not paying bereaved families money that was rightfully theirs.

item.title: UK faces biggest hit to growth from Middle East war, OECD warns
item.description: Outlook underscores economy’s exposure to conflict through reliance on energy imports

item.title: The best exhibitions to see in London this weekend
item.description: The FT’s critics recommend the most compelling 2026 shows, from Cecily Brown and Hurvin Anderson to Tracey Emin to Rose Wylie

item.title: Private credit rating agency hits back at ‘incendiary allegation’ by SEC
item.description: Egan-Jones claims US regulator harmed its business with publication of order this week

item.title: Who Is Alireza Tangsiri, the Latest Iranian Commander Targeted by Israel?
item.description: Israel said it killed the naval commander, Alireza Tangsiri, in an airstrike on Thursday morning. Iran has not commented.

item.title: Queen’s Park is London’s hottest culinary corner
item.description: Have you hit the royal mile?

item.title: How to rescue rules-based trade from WTO gridlock
item.description: Governments need to copy, bypass and borrow from the institution where they can

item.title: Brussels targets big porn platforms over child safety concerns
item.description: Pornhub, Stripchat, XNXX and XVideos accused of inadequate checks on users’ ages

item.title: Fertiliser price surge due to Iran war coincides with US planting season
item.description: Donald Trump set to meet with top industry figures in critical political constituency

item.title: The EU economy’s slow-burning problem
item.description: Sluggish private R&D spending across Europe is hurting competitiveness, data suggests

item.title: How conflict is driving hunger
item.description: The Swede who runs the World Food Programme on its hardest year for decades

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Zimbabwe says 15 nationals killed after being lured to fight for Russia
item.description: Zimbabwe says it is engaging the Russian government to repatriate bodies and bring home those still alive.

item.title: How long will Israel stay in Lebanon?
item.description: The threat of another occupation is tearing at the fabric of Lebanese society

item.title: The end of the world’s longest-running Maoist insurgency
item.description: A report from one of the Naxalites’ final redoubts

item.title: UK now 'ready' to seize Russian shadow fleet ships, Healey says
item.description: Forces are now approved to board sanctioned vessels if required, after ministers identified a legal basis in January.

item.title: Central bank governance reform for the lols
item.description: End the Fed?

item.title: Donald Trump says he is close to a deal with Iran
item.description: Almost no one believes that talks are getting anywhere

item.title: It turns out that legalising gambling is bad
item.description: This has shocked us to our core

item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Q&A with FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens, today at 1pm GMT

item.title: Advantage Iran
item.description: A month of bombing has achieved nothing. Will Donald Trump escalate, or talk?

item.title: Europe should think twice before weakening its merger rules
item.description: A strict competition policy is not the barrier to bigger firms

item.title: The case against energy bail-outs
item.description: As war rages in Iran, governments must not repeat the mistakes of 2022

item.title: FirstFT: Scott Bessent looks at the Federal Reserve’s oversight
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: SpaceX boosts IPO ambitions, and a $22bn merger in the insurance industry

item.title: Findlay felt capable of killing attacker but says 'I'm not a scrapper'
item.description: The Scottish Conservative leader discussed his visceral reaction to an acid attack in 2015 with the BBC's Scotcast.

item.title: Ex-Taiwan presidential hopeful sentenced to 17 years for corruption
item.description: Ko Wen-je's supporters had previously described the charges against him as politically motivated.

item.title: NS&I set to pay UK savers hundreds of millions in missing payments
item.description: Bereaved families particularly affected due to ‘tracing’ issues at state-owned savings bank

item.title: US inflation will surge to 4.2% on energy shock, warns OECD
item.description: Middle East war to push American price growth to ‘highest in G7’

item.title: OpenAI puts erotic chatbot plans on hold ‘indefinitely’
item.description: Decision follows staff and investor concerns about sexual AI content

item.title: India Appears Sidelined as Pakistan Tries to Play Peacemaker in Iran
item.description: Hours before a call between President Trump and India’s prime minister, American officials urged India to focus on shared goals and ignore differences.

item.title: Next warns Middle East war could push up prices
item.description: UK fashion retailer has already accounted for £15mn of additional costs from higher fuel and air freight charges

item.title: Equitable and Corebridge in talks to create $22bn life insurance giant
item.description: Plans come as private capital groups have hitched their growth to managing assets held by US insurers

item.title: In fragmented Britain, who can say what counts as ‘good’ performance?
item.description: We can no longer use historical yardsticks for local election success

item.title: Arrests of Palestine Action protesters to resume, Met says
item.description: The Met says it remains a criminal offence to support the banned group and it "must enforce the law".

item.title: In Denmark, It’s All About the Pigs
item.description: In Denmark’s election, it was local issues, not Greenland or foreign policy, that counted. That hurt the prime minister, Mette Frederiksen.

item.title: Cuban Patients Are Dying Because of U.S. Blockade, Doctors Say
item.description: Cuban health care was once the pride of the island. Now the U.S. oil blockade is upending even basic medical care.

item.title: In Rural Ukraine, Basic Health Care Is a Casualty of War
item.description: Elderly people in isolated villages are going without medicine. One woman said she hadn’t seen a doctor in four years.

item.title: 'We are being choked': Philippines transport workers strike over fuel costs
item.description: It comes as a ship carrying barrels of Russian crude oil arrived in the Philippines earlier this week.

item.title: Chris Mason: How will the UK respond to US court ruling on social media?
item.description: Will the decision of a Los Angeles jury have an impact on the UK government as it considers potential future regulations on social media, writes the BBC's political editor.

item.title: Rescuers Work to Save a 32-Foot Humpback Whale Trapped in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The whale has been stuck for days in shallow waters of the Baltic Sea, as rescuers prepared to make another attempt to free it.

item.title: Crypto donations to UK parties to be banned
item.description: Ministers are also capping donations of British citizens living abroad, in response to a review.

item.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Trump confirms May meeting with Xi Jinping as Iran war forces postponement
item.description: Trump's delayed meeting with Xi Jinping will be the first visit to China by a US president since 2017.

item.title: What to look out for in European World Cup play-offs
item.description: The World Cup play-offs start on Thursday with four European spaces up for grabs for this summer's tournament.

item.title: In one of country's poorest states, Venezuelans hope for post-Maduro boom
item.description: Ione Wells travels to Sucre, where a lack of investment has hampered locals' prospects.

item.title: Nigel Farage hints at return for fellow Essex MP
item.description: Nigel Farage says he hopes things are "ironed out" for James McMurdock MP.

item.title: Australia bans Iranian tourists with valid visas for six months
item.description: Critics say the move, which comes after Iranian footballers were granted asylum, is a "moral failure".

item.title: Chinese Education Influencer’s Sudden Death Prompts Grief, and Reflection
item.description: The influencer, Zhang Xuefeng, was known for no-nonsense, some said cynical, advice about how to win in China’s educational rat race. He died at 41.

item.title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
item.description: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.

item.title: How U.S.A.I.D. Birth Control Meant for Africa Was Ruined
item.description: The Trump administration had options for offloading contraceptives once destined for Africa, a newly obtained memo shows. Instead, it has let them collect dust and go bad.

item.title: Ministers hope red tape cuts will speed up decision-making
item.description: The Conservatives said the changes were being made by a government "stuck in consultation paralysis".

item.title: Republicans in Congress Fret Over Trump Administration’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or timeline.

item.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
item.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

item.title: Former AFL player becomes first to come out as gay
item.description: Leigh Ryswyk, who played one game for the Brisbane Lions in 2005, shared the news on a radio interview.

item.title: Canada’s Supreme Court Hears Case on Ability to Suspend Constitutional Rights
item.description: Quebec’s ban on religious symbols — and a measure that suspends constitutional rights — are being tested in a case with far-reaching repercussions.

item.title: Peers defy government by pushing for UK social media ban for under-16s
item.description: The government is consulting on whether the UK should follow Australia by introducing a ban.

item.title: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
item.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

item.title: A right-wing populist party made big poll gains - and it's shaking up Australian politics
item.description: Pauline Hanson's One Nation is riding a wave of popularity driven by voters weary of mainstream parties.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
item.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

item.title: Reform MSP backs Scottish leader Offord after his crude joke condemned
item.description: The party's leader in Scotland has admitted making a "clumsy" remark at a rugby club dinner in 2018.

item.title: UN votes to recognise enslavement of Africans as 'gravest crime against humanity'
item.description: The landmark resolution calls for an apology and contributions to a reparations fund, without specifying an amount.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Are Talking About Ways to End the War: What to Know
item.description: The United States is discussing ways to end hostilities with Iran, even as the Pentagon dispatches more troops.

item.title: Europe’s populist right should be outvoted rather than ostracised
item.description: Imagining there’s no fascists is a bad strategy

item.title: The Revolutionary Guards are taking over Iran
item.description: They now appear to run both the state and the war

item.title: Government backs five-council plan for Essex
item.description: The network of 15 councils in Essex will be abolished and replaced.

item.title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, Is Enthroned
item.description: Sarah Mullally was installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.

item.title: Doctors announce six-day strike in England as talks break down
item.description: The walkout over jobs and pay is one of the longest yet in the dispute, and will begin on 7 April in England.

item.title: Mexico must unleash its private sector
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum’s biggest problem is weak investment and growth, not Donald Trump

item.title: Four arrested in Germany after Channel smuggling gang raids
item.description: Europol said more than 450 officers from the UK, Germany and Belgium were involved in the operation.

item.title: Senegal appeal to be heard 'as swiftly as possible'
item.description: The Court of Arbitration for Sport (Cas) says it will rule on Senegal's appeal against being stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations title "as swiftly as possible".

item.title: Inside Trump’s Secret Deal to Deport Migrants to Cameroon
item.description: In Cameroon, the Trump administration found a partner it could pressure into accepting covertly deported migrants.

item.title: South Africa's police chief to appear in court over controversial health contract
item.description: South African authorities declined to disclose the charges he will face when he stands in the dock.

item.title: No 10 refuses to say if Mandelson texts were lost in top aide's phone theft
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's former chief of staff Morgan McSweeney's phone may have contained vital information.

item.title: Council tax bills in England to rise in April - see how yours compares
item.description: It will rise by 4.9% on average across England from next month, new figures show.

item.title: Sexual Misconduct Report Leaves I.C.C.’s Path Ahead Unclear
item.description: In a report obtained by The New York Times, a panel of judges found that evidence of sexual misconduct by the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court left room for “reasonable doubt.”

item.title: Saudi Pro League, the US or Europe - what are Salah's options?
item.description: Liverpool reporter Aadam Patel analyses what Mohamed Salah's next move might be following the forward's announcement that he is to leave the club this summer.

item.title: Haaland donates £100,000 Viking book to home town
item.description: Erling Haaland donates a rare 16th-Century book of Viking sagas, worth £100,000, to be displayed in the library in the Norwegian town of Bryne, where he grew up.

item.title: Musketeer d'Artagnan's remains believed found under Dutch church
item.description: D'Artagnan was killed during the Siege of Maastricht in 1673 and later immortalised in the stories of Alexandre Dumas.

item.title: Ukraine Finally Got Battlefield Momentum. Now Comes a Russian Offensive.
item.description: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.

item.title: Ex-justice minister fined for possessing illegal drugs
item.description: Crispin Blunt pleaded guilty after cannabis and crystal meth were found in his Surrey home.

item.title: Estonia and Latvia say territories hit by stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: The incidents occurred on the same night that Ukraine launched a massive attack on Russia's port of Ust-Luga, near the Estonian border.

item.title: Indian singer's drowning was accidental - Singapore coroner
item.description: The coroner ruled that there was no evidence of foul play in the death of iconic singer Zubeen Garg.

item.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
item.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Brazil's Bolsonaro gets temporary house arrest for ill-health
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was moved from his cell to hospital earlier this month with pneumonia.

item.title: Dozens of bodies, mostly infants, discovered in Kenya mass grave
item.description: Some of the 32 bodies, including 25 children, are believed to have come from local hospitals and mortuaries.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

item.title: 'Major player' in alleged mistaken identity kidnap of Sydney grandfather charged
item.description: The 23-year-old is the third man to be charged in connection with the death of Chris Baghsarian.

item.title: Cheap Drones Remain Wild Card in Iran war
item.description: Stopping Iran’s production of drones is critical to opening the Strait of Hormuz and halting its attacks on Gulf nations. But can it be done?

item.title: The world’s most unaffordable housing is not where you think
item.description: Asia’s great cities will founder without more reasonably priced homes

item.title: Marcos promises 'flow of oil' as Philippines declares energy emergency
item.description: The President says the country will procure one million barrels of oil to add to the current stock.

item.title: Danish PM fails to secure majority in party's weakest election showing since 1903
item.description: The party, which has been in power since 2019, gained the most votes but faces tough talks on forming a new government.

item.title: Where does Salah rank among greatest Premier League forwards?
item.description: As Mohamed Salah announces he will leave Liverpool this summer, BBC Sport asks you to rank the best forwards in Premier League history.

item.title: Journalists at Australia's national broadcaster strike for first time in 20 years
item.description: Staff have walked out for 24 hours over a pay rise offer that was below inflation.

item.title: Has banning phones improved performance at Dutch schools?
item.description: Two years ago the Netherlands banned phones in schools. Now the government wants to go further, pushing to restrict social media for under‑16s.

item.title: Sara Duterte impeachment hearings start as political death match heats up
item.description: The Philippines' VP faces another impeachment round with her 2028 presidential run on the line.

item.title: BTS comeback concert on Netflix draws 18.4m global viewers
item.description: The livestreamed show saw the band perform together for the first time since October 2022.

item.title: Social media bans and digital curfews to be trialled on UK teenagers
item.description: The government will interview the young people and their parents before and after they try the limits to assess their impact.

item.title: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
item.description: Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured.

item.title: Death toll from Colombian military plane crash rises to 69 as search ends
item.description: The Hercules military transport plane crashed minutes after take-off near Colombia's southern border with Peru.

item.title: Salah to leave Liverpool at end of season
item.description: The Egypt forward says Liverpool fans "gave me the best time of my life" and "stood by me in the toughest times".

item.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
item.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

item.title: China’s government both drives and constrains the rise of AI
item.description: Officials want to lead the world in the new technology, but are still wary of disruption

item.title: German outcry over deep fake porn targeting actress prompts bid to change law
item.description: Collien Fernandes has accused her ex-husband of spreading sexualised images of her online.

item.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
item.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

item.title: 'Truly magical woman': Tributes to Married at First Sight's Mel Schilling
item.description: The TV dating coach is remembered as "a radiant, shining light" following her death at the age of 54.

item.title: Everyday life in Asia is being upended by Iran war fuel crisis
item.description: Asia relies heavily on oil and gas from the Gulf, and shortages and higher prices are starting to bite.

item.title: Israel says it will take control of large buffer zone in southern Lebanon
item.description: Thousands of displaced Lebanese residents will not be allowed to return home until northern Israel is safe, Israel's defence minister says.

item.title: Marco Rubio testifies in former housemate's Maduro lobbying trial
item.description: Rubio's former housemate is charged with illegally trying to influence the first Trump administration on behalf of the Venezuelan government.

item.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
item.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

item.title: Reeves plans energy bill help for those 'who need it most'
item.description: The US-Israel war with Iran is having an impact on costs domestically, with oil and gas prices soaring.

item.title: Heat pumps for all new homes and plug-in solar in green tech drive
item.description: Solar panels that can be plugged in at home could be available to buy in supermarkets in the coming months.

item.title: Botswana prepares to take an even bigger gamble on diamonds
item.description: The African country wants to own more of De Beers, the firm that helped it to escape poverty

item.title: Irish government cuts excise duty on diesel and petrol
item.description: Duty on diesel will be reduced by 20 cent and petrol will be cut by 15 cent from midnight on Wednesday.

item.title: Lib Dems fix communities, says Davey at election campaign launch
item.description: The Lib Dem leader highlights policies on the NHS and energy bills ahead of council polls in England.

item.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
item.description: Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations.

item.title: Elon Musk's Starlink blocked from operating in Namibia
item.description: Namibia's regulator noted that Starlink's subsidiary is not locally owned.

item.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness
item.description: Two pregnant women tell the BBC's Will Grant of their hopes and fears as their nation is mired in crisis.

item.title: UK must back North Sea oil and gas drilling, says trade body
item.description: The group says the country "urgently" needs to produce its own oil and gas to secure supplies.

item.title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
item.description: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.

item.title: UK universities flock to India - but will they succeed?
item.description: Nine UK universities are setting up campuses in India, but enrolment is expected to be modest initially.

item.title: Iwobi named on Football Black List alongside Carter and Eze
item.description: England defender Jess Carter, Arsenal attacker Eberechi Eze and Fulham midfielder Alex Iwobi are on the 2025 Football Black List.

item.title: Giorgia Meloni’s big setback in Italy
item.description: Her failed referendum gamble hints at growing dissatisfaction with her government

item.title: Referendum defeat leaves Italy's Meloni looking more vulnerable
item.description: Meloni vowed to press on after losing a vote on constitutional reform many turned into a referendum on her government.

item.title: Donald Trump’s latest climbdown suggests he wants to end the war
item.description: The divide between Israel and America is widening

item.title: The War Room newsletter: Trump is scrambling for options in Iran
item.description: “To end the war on current terms would look like defeat [for Trump] to most of the world,” writes Shashank Joshi, our defence editor

item.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
item.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

item.title: Why the number of Islamic schools in Canada is soaring
item.description: Some Muslim parents worry about their children losing touch with their faith

item.title: Could Iranian missiles reach London and Paris?
item.description: Some experts say Iran may be able to launch missiles to hit London, but they would likely be shot down long before arriving and the risk is low.

item.title: Nigerian heritage key to my success - Itauma
item.description: Moses Itauma, one of heavyweight boxing's brightest prospects, says he found the answers to questions about his identity on a trip to his father's village in Nigeria.

item.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
item.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.
